summary: Full backend + frontend testing of Year-in-Books PUBLIC SHARE feature (iteration 6). 25 new pytest cases for share endpoints (auth gates, year validation, full lifecycle: create -> idempotent POST -> public view -> view-count increment -> bad token 404 -> revoke -> 404 after revoke -> double-revoke no-op) + 6 regression checks (auth/me, books, stats, digest settings, year-in-books GET, year-in-books email) ALL PASS (25/25). Re-ran prior 64 backend tests (test_year_in_books, test_digest, test_new_features) ALL PASS (64/64). Frontend e2e via Playwright: share-year-recap button, share-dialog-overlay with url/copy/open/view-count/revoke/close sub-testids, label switch 'Share this recap' -> 'Manage share link' after reload, close-via-X and close-via-outside-click, public page at /share/yib/{token} (no auth context, no Navbar logout, email NOT leaked in DOM, document.title set to '<name>'s <year> in books — Shelfsort', CTA -> /login), bad token -> not-found state, revoke flow -> label reverts and public link returns not-found, plus regressions for /library/stats, /library/author/x, /account/digest — ALL PASS.

issues: Dev-mode hydration warning: 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>' — caused by Emergent's x-source dev wrappers injecting <span> inside <select><option>. Not a functional bug; only appears in dev/preview build.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 3110 lines — well over the 700-line guideline. Should be split into routers (auth, books, stats, year_in_books, share, digest) for maintainability. Not blocking.
Public share endpoint correctly strips book_id from first_book/last_book and never exposes email — verified explicitly via DOM scan and JSON-body scan in tests.
POST /share idempotency confirmed: second POST returns the same token (matches existing year_in_books_shares unique index on (user_id, year)).
View counter increments on every public GET (tested baseline+2 after two requests). last_viewed_at is updated.
DELETE /share is idempotent-safe: returns {revoked:true} on first call, {revoked:false} on subsequent (no 404, good UX).
test_credentials: Tests register fresh users via POST /api/auth/register and seed Mongo books + reading_activity, then clean up. No persistent credentials needed.
seed_data_creation: Per-test ephemeral Mongo seeding: 1 user (TEST_/test_ prefix email), 1 book (Harry Potter / Fanfiction, year 2025), 1 reading_activity entry. All cleaned in module teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Year-in-Books public sharing is fully functional. All share endpoints (/api/year-in-books/{year}/share GET/POST/DELETE + /api/public/year/{token}) work as spec'd including idempotency, view-counter, revocation, and privacy (no email/book_id leak). Frontend dialog (share-dialog-overlay) + public page (PublicYearInBooks.jsx) verified. include_router move did not break any prior endpoints. test_year_share.py is the canonical regression file for this feature.
rca of the issue: N/A — feature works as specified, no bugs found.
